Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary shall make grants for services for the prevention and treatment of type I diabetes in children, and for research in innovative approaches to such services. Such grants may be made to children’s hospitals; grantees under and other federally qualified health centers; State and local health departments; and other appropriate public or nonprofit private entities.”Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary, directly or through grants, shall provide for research into the prevention and cure of Type I diabetes.1So in original. Probably should not be capitalized.1

children.heading: Transferred funds
children.label: 1 Transferred funds
children.text: Notwithstanding , from the amounts appropriated in such section for each of fiscal years 1998 through 2002, $30,000,000 is hereby transferred and made available in such fiscal year for grants under this section.section 1397dd(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: $70,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2001 and 2002 (which shall be combined with amounts transferred under paragraph (1) for each such fiscal years);

children.label: C
children.text: $150,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2004 through 2017;

children.label: D
children.text: $150,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2023, to remain available until expended;

children.label: E
children.text: $155,619,196 for fiscal year 2024, to remain available until expended;

children.label: F
children.text: $159,228,188 for fiscal year 2025, to remain available until expended;

children.label: G
children.text: $200,000,000 for fiscal year 2026, to remain available until expended; and

children.label: H
children.text: $50,410,959 for the period beginning on , and ending on , to remain available until expended.October 1, 2026December 31, 2026
